verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is secreted by epididymal epithelium into the epididymal lumen, where it binds stably to the dorsal/postacrosomal region of the sperm plasma membrane during epididymal transit. This association is wash-resistant (a tightly bound population persists on ejaculated sperm) and the protein then functions at the sperm surface during sperm-egg fusion. Thus the protein body reaches an outer-leaflet PM location via post-translational deposition onto a partner cell surface, fitting stable_surface_attachment.
